projectDescription: A Python CLI and library that lets any LLM actually watch a video by extracting scene-aware, deduplicated frames and a transcript from a URL or local file — all processed locally.
keyFeatures: Scene-change detection extracts only visually meaningful frames (not fixed-interval sampling)
Sliding-window deduplication prevents repeated shots from being sent to the model multiple times
Automatic transcription via existing subtitles (sidecar .srt/.vtt or embedded stream) with Whisper fallback
Supports both remote URLs (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, etc. via yt-dlp) and local video files
Optional full audio track extraction (audio.m4a) for models that can listen
MANIFEST.txt output that any LLM can read to understand the video
Knowledge-base integration (--kb) to save analysis as dated markdown notes (e.g. Obsidian)
Tunable dedup report (report.html) for visualising every keep/drop decision
Fully local — nothing is uploaded to any cloud service
Login-gated video support via Netscape cookie file (--cookies)
publicApiSurface: python -m claude_real_video <source> [options] — module alias for CLI
process(src, out_dir, *, scene, fps_floor, max_frames, lang, cookies, do_transcribe, dedup_threshold, dedup_window, keep_audio, report, why) -> Result — Python API
Result dataclass: out_dir, video, duration, frames_dir, frame_count, extracted_frames, transcript_path, manifest_path, transcript_note, audio_path, report_path
dedup_frames(frames_dir, threshold, window, max_frames, dropped_dir) -> tuple[int, list[dict]]
